---
title: ""
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

```{=html}
<div class="page-header container">
  <p class="page-header-label">Publications</p>
  <h1 class="page-header-title">Selected Work</h1>
  <p class="page-header-sub">
    Sixteen years of research, one plot. Every published work below — articles,
    law reviews, book chapters, and the book — organized into the four threads
    that run through the record. Hover any point for the citation; the curated
    highlights follow.
  </p>
</div>

<div class="container" style="max-width: 960px;">
  <div id="pubchart-card" style="background:#ffffff; border:1px solid #e8e4df; border-radius:10px; padding:1.5rem 1.25rem 1rem;">
    <div id="pubchart" style="overflow-x:auto; -webkit-overflow-scrolling:touch;"></div>
    <p style="margin:0.6rem 0 0; color:#888; font-size:0.8rem;">
      <span id="pubchart-swipe-hint" style="display:none;">Swipe the chart to see the full timeline. </span>
      Peer-reviewed articles, law reviews, book chapters, and books, 2009–2025.
      Full citations in the lists below and in the CV.
    </p>
  </div>
</div>

<div id="pub-tooltip" style="position:absolute; display:none; pointer-events:none; z-index:50;
  background:#3C3C3C; color:#ffffff; border-radius:6px; padding:0.55rem 0.7rem;
  font-size:0.8rem; line-height:1.45; max-width:320px; box-shadow:0 4px 14px rgba(0,0,0,0.18);"></div>

<script>
(function () {
  // thread palette — validated categorical hues (light surface)
  const THREADS = [
    { key: "aging",    label: "Aging & the Law",                    color: "#8a4090" },
    { key: "juvenile", label: "Juvenile & Emerging Adult Justice",  color: "#c75b3f" },
    { key: "courts",   label: "Courts, Data & Communication",       color: "#0d8f76" },
    { key: "evidence", label: "Evidence & Empirical Legal Studies", color: "#9a7d10" }
  ];

  const PUBS = [
    // Aging & the Law
    { y: 2025, t: "aging", title: "Aging and the Criminal Legal System: Community Services, Courts, Corrections, and Policy", venue: "APA, Division 41 book series", type: "Book" },
    { y: 2023, t: "aging", title: "Aging Prisoners", venue: "Handbook of Developmental Psychology and Law", type: "Book chapter" },
    { y: 2021, t: "aging", title: "Legal Responsibility Among the Young and the Elderly", venue: "Annual Review of Law and Social Science", type: "Article" },
    { y: 2021, t: "aging", title: "Creating Opportunities for End-of-Life Planning", venue: "Journal of Aging and Social Policy", type: "Article" },
    { y: 2018, t: "aging", title: "Measuring Older Adult Confidence in the Courts and Law Enforcement", venue: "Criminal Justice Policy Review", type: "Article" },
    { y: 2018, t: "aging", title: "A Review of Federal and State Compassionate Release Laws", venue: "Psychology, Public Policy, and Law", type: "Article" },
    { y: 2015, t: "aging", title: "“Now Dear, What Do You Remember?” Patronizing Communication Toward Older Adults and Memory Performance", venue: "American Journal of Forensic Psychology", type: "Article" },
    { y: 2015, t: "aging", title: "Age and Lineup Type Differences in the Own-Race Bias", venue: "Psychology, Crime, and Law", type: "Article" },
    { y: 2015, t: "aging", title: "Elders and the Justice System", venue: "APA Handbook of Forensic Psychology", type: "Book chapter" },
    { y: 2014, t: "aging", title: "Misinformation Effects in Older Versus Younger Adults: A Meta-analysis and Review", venue: "The Elderly Eyewitness in Court", type: "Book chapter" },
    { y: 2014, t: "aging", title: "Differing Perspectives on Older Adult Caregiving", venue: "Journal of Applied Gerontology", type: "Article" },
    { y: 2012, t: "aging", title: "Potential for Self-reporting of Older Adult Maltreatment", venue: "Elder Law Journal", type: "Law review" },
    { y: 2009, t: "aging", title: "Assuming Responsibility: Am I a Caregiver?", venue: "Journal of Empirical Legal Studies", type: "Article" },
    // Juvenile & Emerging Adult Justice
    { y: 2023, t: "juvenile", title: "Electronically Monitored Youth: Stigma and Negative Social Functioning", venue: "Crime & Delinquency", type: "Article" },
    { y: 2022, t: "juvenile", title: "Meeting the Needs of Emerging Adults in the Justice System", venue: "Trends in State Courts", type: "Article" },
    { y: 2022, t: "juvenile", title: "Defense, Disrespect, and #Deadly: Youth Violence and a Hospital-based Prevention Program", venue: "Journal of Qualitative Criminology and Criminal Justice", type: "Article" },
    { y: 2021, t: "juvenile", title: "Variations in Victim Presence in Restorative Conferencing Programs", venue: "Victims and Offenders", type: "Article" },
    { y: 2021, t: "juvenile", title: "Perceptions of Violence in Justice-Involved Youth", venue: "Surgery Open Science", type: "Article" },
    { y: 2021, t: "juvenile", title: "Drug Testing Policies and Practices in Juvenile Pretrial Diversion", venue: "Justice Evaluation Journal", type: "Article" },
    { y: 2019, t: "juvenile", title: "The Application of Risk-Needs-Responsivity Programming in Juvenile Diversion", venue: "Criminal Justice and Behavior", type: "Article" },
    { y: 2018, t: "juvenile", title: "The Impact of Victimization and Mental Health on Recidivism for Early System-Involved Juvenile Offenders", venue: "Law and Human Behavior", type: "Article" },
    { y: 2018, t: "juvenile", title: "Absenteeism Interventions: An Approach for Common Measurement", venue: "Justice Evaluation Journal", type: "Article" },
    { y: 2018, t: "juvenile", title: "Risk Factor Profile of Youth Incarcerated for Child-to-Parent Violence", venue: "Journal of Criminal Justice", type: "Article" },
    { y: 2018, t: "juvenile", title: "Child-to-Parent Violence: Empirical Findings, Legal Issues, and Future Research", venue: "Contemporary Issues in Criminology", type: "Book chapter" },
    { y: 2015, t: "juvenile", title: "Using Mail Surveys to Assess Perceptions Regarding Parental Involvement Laws", venue: "“There Ought to be a Law!” (Miller, Ed.)", type: "Book chapter" },
    { y: 2010, t: "juvenile", title: "Assessing School and Individual-Level Predictors of Weapons Reporting", venue: "Youth Violence and Juvenile Justice", type: "Article" },
    // Courts, Data & Communication
    { y: 2025, t: "courts", title: "Cultivating a Court’s Data STORY: A Guide for Court Leaders", venue: "Trends in State Courts", type: "Article" },
    { y: 2018, t: "courts", title: "Forty Years of Law and Human Behavior: A Content Analysis", venue: "Scientometrics", type: "Article" },
    { y: 2017, t: "courts", title: "Public Health Framing and Attribution: The First Lady’s Remarks and News Coverage on Childhood Obesity", venue: "Cogent Social Sciences", type: "Article" },
    { y: 2013, t: "courts", title: "Let’s Discuss: How Teaching About Discussions Can Lead to Better Student Outcomes", venue: "Journal of Scholarship of Teaching and Learning", type: "Article" },
    // Evidence & Empirical Legal Studies
    { y: 2022, t: "evidence", title: "Epidemiological Criminology and COVID: Violent Crime and Emergency Department Admissions", venue: "Trauma Care", type: "Article" },
    { y: 2016, t: "evidence", title: "Problem Signs in Law School: Fostering Attorney Well-being Early in Professional Training", venue: "International Journal of Law and Psychiatry", type: "Article" },
    { y: 2015, t: "evidence", title: "Predicting Outcomes in Investment Treaty Arbitration", venue: "Duke Law Journal", type: "Law review" },
    { y: 2014, t: "evidence", title: "Disputed Paraphilia Diagnosis and Legal Decision Making", venue: "Psychology, Public Policy and Law", type: "Article" },
    { y: 2012, t: "evidence", title: "Can the Courts Tell an Ear from an Eye? Legal Approaches to Voice Identification Evidence", venue: "Psychology and Law Review", type: "Law review" },
    { y: 2011, t: "evidence", title: "An Empirical Study of Whistleblower Policies in United States Corporate Codes of Ethics", venue: "Whistleblowing and Democratic Values", type: "Book chapter" }
  ];

  const Y0 = 2009, Y1 = 2025;
  const W = 920, PAD_L = 16, PAD_R = 16, PAD_T = 8;
  const LANE_LABEL_H = 32, TICK_H = 30;
  const DOT_R = 6, STACK_GAP = 17;

  const ns = "http://www.w3.org/2000/svg";
  const el = (n, attrs) => {
    const e = document.createElementNS(ns, n);
    for (const k in attrs) e.setAttribute(k, attrs[k]);
    return e;
  };
  const xFor = y => PAD_L + ((y - Y0) / (Y1 - Y0)) * (W - PAD_L - PAD_R);

  // lane heights sized to the deepest stack in each thread
  const stacks = {};
  PUBS.forEach(p => {
    const k = p.t + p.y;
    stacks[k] = (stacks[k] || 0) + 1;
    p.stackIdx = stacks[k] - 1;
  });
  const laneMax = {};
  THREADS.forEach(t => {
    laneMax[t.key] = Math.max(1, ...Object.keys(stacks)
      .filter(k => k.startsWith(t.key)).map(k => stacks[k]));
  });

  let laneY = PAD_T;
  const lanes = {};
  THREADS.forEach(t => {
    const h = LANE_LABEL_H + laneMax[t.key] * STACK_GAP + 8;
    lanes[t.key] = { top: laneY, height: h, ...t };
    laneY += h;
  });
  const H = laneY + TICK_H;

  const svg = el("svg", { viewBox: `0 0 ${W} ${H}`, width: "100%", role: "group",
    "aria-label": "Timeline of publications by year and research thread" });
  svg.style.fontFamily = "inherit";
  // below this width the chart scrolls horizontally instead of shrinking,
  // so the text stays legible on phones
  svg.style.minWidth = "680px";
  svg.style.display = "block";

  // hairline year gridlines + ticks (solid, recessive)
  for (let y = Y0; y <= Y1; y++) {
    const x = xFor(y);
    svg.appendChild(el("line", { x1: x, y1: PAD_T, x2: x, y2: laneY, stroke: "#f0ece7", "stroke-width": 1 }));
    if (y % 2 === 1) {
      const tick = el("text", { x: x, y: laneY + 20, "text-anchor": "middle", fill: "#777", "font-size": 13.5 });
      tick.style.fontVariantNumeric = "tabular-nums";
      tick.textContent = y;
      svg.appendChild(tick);
    }
  }

  // lane separators + labels (the identity channel — text in ink, swatch carries color)
  THREADS.forEach((t, i) => {
    const L = lanes[t.key];
    if (i > 0) svg.appendChild(el("line", { x1: PAD_L, y1: L.top, x2: W - PAD_R, y2: L.top, stroke: "#eee9e4", "stroke-width": 1 }));
    svg.appendChild(el("circle", { cx: PAD_L + 6, cy: L.top + 16, r: 6, fill: t.color }));
    const lbl = el("text", { x: PAD_L + 19, y: L.top + 21, fill: "#3C3C3C", "font-size": 16, "font-weight": 600 });
    lbl.textContent = t.label;
    svg.appendChild(lbl);
  });

  // dots: series color fill + 2px surface ring; 24px transparent hit target
  const tip = document.getElementById("pub-tooltip");
  const showTip = (p, target) => {
    tip.replaceChildren();
    const strong = document.createElement("strong");
    strong.textContent = p.title;
    const meta = document.createElement("div");
    meta.style.cssText = "color:#d9d4cf; margin-top:0.2rem;";
    meta.textContent = `${p.venue} · ${p.y} · ${p.type}`;
    tip.append(strong, meta);
    const r = target.getBoundingClientRect();
    tip.style.display = "block";
    const tw = tip.offsetWidth;
    let left = window.scrollX + r.left + r.width / 2 - tw / 2;
    left = Math.max(8, Math.min(left, window.scrollX + document.documentElement.clientWidth - tw - 8));
    tip.style.left = left + "px";
    tip.style.top = (window.scrollY + r.top - tip.offsetHeight - 10) + "px";
  };
  const hideTip = () => { tip.style.display = "none"; };

  PUBS.forEach(p => {
    const L = lanes[p.t];
    const cx = xFor(p.y);
    const cy = L.top + L.height - 12 - p.stackIdx * STACK_GAP;
    svg.appendChild(el("circle", { cx, cy, r: DOT_R, fill: L.color, stroke: "#ffffff", "stroke-width": 2 }));
    const hit = el("circle", { cx, cy, r: 12, fill: "transparent", tabindex: 0, role: "img",
      "aria-label": `${p.title}. ${p.venue}, ${p.y}. ${p.type}.` });
    hit.style.cursor = "pointer";
    hit.addEventListener("pointerenter", () => showTip(p, hit));
    hit.addEventListener("pointerleave", hideTip);
    hit.addEventListener("focus", () => showTip(p, hit));
    hit.addEventListener("blur", hideTip);
    svg.appendChild(hit);
  });

  const chartBox = document.getElementById("pubchart");
  chartBox.appendChild(svg);
  const hint = document.getElementById("pubchart-swipe-hint");
  const updateHint = () => {
    hint.style.display = chartBox.scrollWidth > chartBox.clientWidth ? "inline" : "none";
  };
  updateHint();
  window.addEventListener("resize", updateHint);
})();
</script>
```

## Research threads {.section-heading}

```{=html}
<div class="container" style="max-width: 820px;">

  <h3 style="margin-top:2rem;"><span style="display:inline-block; width:11px; height:11px; border-radius:50%; background:#8a4090; margin-right:0.5rem;"></span>Aging &amp; the Law</h3>
  <p style="color:#4b5563;">
    How the legal system meets older adults — as litigants, victims, caregivers,
    and prisoners.
  </p>
  <div class="project-grid" style="margin-bottom:1.2rem;">
    <div class="project-card">
      <span class="project-card-tag">Book · APA, 2025</span>
      <p class="project-card-title">Aging and the Criminal Legal System</p>
      <p class="project-card-desc">
        <em>Community Services, Courts, Corrections, and Policy.</em>
        With E.M. Brank. Division 41 American Psychology-Law book series,
        American Psychological Association. Brings behavioral science research
        to bear on the legal and psychological challenges facing older adults
        across the criminal legal system.
      </p>
      <br>
      <a href="https://www.apa.org" target="_blank" rel="noopener"
         style="font-weight:600; color:#8C6A8D;">
        View at APA →
      </a>
    </div>
  </div>
  <ul class="pub-list" style="list-style:none; padding-left:0; line-height:1.55; font-size:0.88rem;">
    <li style="margin-bottom:1.1rem;">
      Brank, E.M., &amp; <strong>Wylie, L.E.</strong> (2021). Legal
      responsibility among the young and the elderly.
      <em>Annual Review of Law and Social Science.</em>
    </li>
    <li style="margin-bottom:1.1rem;">
      <strong>Wylie, L.E.</strong>, Knutson, A., &amp; Greene, E. (2018). A
      review of federal and state compassionate release laws: Presence,
      utilization, and implications.
      <em>Psychology, Public Policy, and Law.</em>
    </li>
  </ul>

  <h3 style="margin-top:2.5rem;"><span style="display:inline-block; width:11px; height:11px; border-radius:50%; background:#c75b3f; margin-right:0.5rem;"></span>Juvenile &amp; Emerging Adult Justice</h3>
  <p style="color:#4b5563;">
    Diversion, risk and needs, and what actually changes outcomes for young
    people in the system.
  </p>
  <ul class="pub-list" style="list-style:none; padding-left:0; line-height:1.55; font-size:0.88rem;">
    <li style="margin-bottom:1.1rem;">
      Kotlaja, M., &amp; <strong>Wylie, L.E.</strong> (2023). Electronically
      monitored youth: Stigma and negative social functioning.
      <em>Crime &amp; Delinquency.</em>
    </li>
    <li style="margin-bottom:1.1rem;">
      Deal, T., Cienfuegos, A., &amp; <strong>Wylie, L.E.</strong> (2022).
      Meeting the needs of emerging adults in the justice system.
      <em>Trends in State Courts.</em>
    </li>
    <li style="margin-bottom:1.1rem;">
      <strong>Wylie, L.E.</strong>, Clinkinbeard, S., &amp; Hobbs, A. (2019).
      The application of risk-needs-responsivity programming in juvenile
      diversion. <em>Criminal Justice and Behavior.</em>
    </li>
    <li style="margin-bottom:1.1rem;">
      <strong>Wylie, L.E.</strong>, &amp; Rufino, K.A. (2018). The impact of
      victimization and mental health on recidivism for early
      system-involved juvenile offenders. <em>Law and Human Behavior.</em>
    </li>
  </ul>

  <h3 style="margin-top:2.5rem;"><span style="display:inline-block; width:11px; height:11px; border-radius:50%; background:#0d8f76; margin-right:0.5rem;"></span>Courts, Data &amp; Communication</h3>
  <p style="color:#4b5563;">
    Helping courts measure, understand, and tell the story of their own work —
    the thread this website practices in public.
  </p>
  <ul class="pub-list" style="list-style:none; padding-left:0; line-height:1.55; font-size:0.88rem;">
    <li style="margin-bottom:1.1rem;">
      <strong>Wylie, L.E.</strong> (2025). Cultivating a court's data STORY:
      A guide for court leaders to develop storytelling skills and effective
      communications. <em>Trends in State Courts.</em>
    </li>
    <li style="margin-bottom:1.1rem;">
      <strong>Wylie, L.E.</strong>, Hoetger, L.A., Hazen, K., Haby, J., &amp;
      Brank, E.M. (2018). Forty years of <em>Law and Human Behavior</em>: A
      content analysis. <em>Scientometrics.</em>
    </li>
  </ul>

  <h3 style="margin-top:2.5rem;"><span style="display:inline-block; width:11px; height:11px; border-radius:50%; background:#9a7d10; margin-right:0.5rem;"></span>Evidence &amp; Empirical Legal Studies</h3>
  <p style="color:#4b5563;">
    Empirical answers to doctrinal questions — from arbitration outcomes to
    voice identification evidence.
  </p>
  <ul class="pub-list" style="list-style:none; padding-left:0; line-height:1.55; font-size:0.88rem;">
    <li style="margin-bottom:1.1rem;">
      Franck, S., &amp; <strong>Wylie, L.E.</strong> (2015). Predicting
      outcomes in investment treaty arbitration. <em>Duke Law Journal.</em>
    </li>
  </ul>

</div>
```

## Citation impact {.section-heading}

```{=html}
<div class="container" style="max-width: 960px;">
  <p style="color:#4b5563; max-width:820px;">
    How the field has used the work: the twenty most-cited publications,
    colored by research thread. Roughly 880 citations across the published
    record, led by the threads' crossover pieces.
  </p>
  <div id="citechart-card" style="background:#ffffff; border:1px solid #e8e4df; border-radius:10px; padding:1.5rem 1.25rem 1rem;">
    <div id="citechart" style="overflow-x:auto; -webkit-overflow-scrolling:touch;"></div>
    <p style="margin:0.6rem 0 0; color:#888; font-size:0.8rem;">
      <span id="citechart-swipe-hint" style="display:none;">Swipe the chart to see full bars. </span>
      Citation counts from Google Scholar, July 2026. Duplicate Scholar records
      merged (counts summed). Fourteen further publications have 9 or fewer
      citations each — about 50 combined — and the newest works are not yet
      cited. Project reports and briefs are excluded.
    </p>
  </div>
</div>

<script>
(function () {
  const THREADS = {
    aging:    { label: "Aging & the Law",                    key: "Aging & the Law",      color: "#8a4090" },
    juvenile: { label: "Juvenile & Emerging Adult Justice",  key: "Juvenile Justice",     color: "#c75b3f" },
    courts:   { label: "Courts, Data & Communication",       key: "Courts & Data",        color: "#0d8f76" },
    evidence: { label: "Evidence & Empirical Legal Studies", key: "Evidence & Empirical", color: "#9a7d10" }
  };

  const CITES = [
    { n: 159, t: "evidence", short: "Investment treaty arbitration",       title: "Predicting Outcomes in Investment Treaty Arbitration", venue: "Duke Law Journal", y: 2015, note: "two Scholar records merged (130 + 29)" },
    { n: 100, t: "aging",    short: "Misinformation in older adults",      title: "Misinformation Effects in Older Versus Younger Adults: A Meta-analysis and Review", venue: "The Elderly Eyewitness in Court", y: 2014 },
    { n: 98,  t: "juvenile", short: "Child-to-parent violence",            title: "Risk Factor Profile of Youth Incarcerated for Child-to-Parent Violence", venue: "Journal of Criminal Justice", y: 2018 },
    { n: 60,  t: "juvenile", short: "Victimization & juvenile recidivism", title: "The Impact of Victimization and Mental Health Symptoms on Recidivism for Early System-Involved Juvenile Offenders", venue: "Law and Human Behavior", y: 2018 },
    { n: 35,  t: "aging",    short: "Compassionate release laws",          title: "Extraordinary and Compelling: The Use of Compassionate Release Laws in the United States", venue: "Psychology, Public Policy, and Law", y: 2018 },
    { n: 34,  t: "evidence", short: "Law student well-being",              title: "Problem Signs in Law School: Fostering Attorney Well-being Early in Professional Training", venue: "International Journal of Law and Psychiatry", y: 2016 },
    { n: 33,  t: "juvenile", short: "Risk–needs programming in diversion", title: "The Application of Risk–Needs Programming in a Juvenile Diversion Program", venue: "Criminal Justice and Behavior", y: 2019 },
    { n: 31,  t: "aging",    short: "Own-race bias in lineups",            title: "Age and Lineup Type Differences in the Own-Race Bias", venue: "Psychology, Crime & Law", y: 2015 },
    { n: 31,  t: "evidence", short: "Whistleblower policies",              title: "An Empirical Study of Whistleblower Policies in United States Corporate Codes of Ethics", venue: "Whistleblowing and Democratic Values", y: 2011 },
    { n: 28,  t: "evidence", short: "Voice identification evidence",       title: "Can the Courts Tell an Ear from an Eye? Legal Approaches to Voice Identification Evidence", venue: "Law & Psychology Review", y: 2013 },
    { n: 28,  t: "juvenile", short: "School weapons reporting",            title: "Assessing School and Student Predictors of Weapons Reporting", venue: "Youth Violence and Juvenile Justice", y: 2010 },
    { n: 26,  t: "aging",    short: "Older adult caregiving",              title: "Differing Perspectives on Older Adult Caregiving", venue: "Journal of Applied Gerontology", y: 2016 },
    { n: 25,  t: "juvenile", short: "Absenteeism interventions",           title: "Absenteeism Interventions: An Approach for Common Definitions in Statewide Program Evaluations", venue: "Justice Evaluation Journal", y: 2018 },
    { n: 25,  t: "courts",   short: "Teaching with discussions",           title: "Let's Discuss: Teaching Students About Discussions", venue: "Journal of Scholarship of Teaching and Learning", y: 2013 },
    { n: 22,  t: "courts",   short: "Law and Human Behavior at 40",        title: "Four Decades of the Journal Law and Human Behavior: A Content Analysis", venue: "Scientometrics", y: 2018 },
    { n: 22,  t: "aging",    short: "Am I a caregiver?",                   title: "Assuming Elder Care Responsibility: Am I a Caregiver?", venue: "Journal of Empirical Legal Studies", y: 2009 },
    { n: 19,  t: "aging",    short: "Elder maltreatment self-report",      title: "Potential for Self-reporting of Older Adult Maltreatment: An Empirical Examination", venue: "Elder Law Journal", y: 2012 },
    { n: 18,  t: "evidence", short: "Paraphilia diagnoses & the law",      title: "Disputed Paraphilia Diagnoses and Legal Decision Making: A Case Law Survey", venue: "Psychology, Public Policy, and Law", y: 2014 },
    { n: 16,  t: "aging",    short: "Older adults' confidence in courts",  title: "Measuring Older Adult Confidence in the Courts and Law Enforcement", venue: "Criminal Justice Policy Review", y: 2018 },
    { n: 13,  t: "courts",   short: "Childhood obesity framing",           title: "Public Health Framing and Attribution: Analysis of the First Lady's Remarks and News Coverage on Childhood Obesity", venue: "Cogent Social Sciences", y: 2017 }
  ];

  const W = 920, LABEL_W = 258, VAL_W = 52, PAD_T = 34, ROW_H = 27, BAR_H = 15;
  const H = PAD_T + CITES.length * ROW_H + 30;
  const MAXV = Math.max(...CITES.map(d => d.n));
  const x0 = LABEL_W + 8, x1 = W - VAL_W;
  const xFor = v => x0 + (v / MAXV) * (x1 - x0);

  const ns = "http://www.w3.org/2000/svg";
  const el = (n, attrs) => {
    const e = document.createElementNS(ns, n);
    for (const k in attrs) e.setAttribute(k, attrs[k]);
    return e;
  };

  const svg = el("svg", { viewBox: `0 0 ${W} ${H}`, width: "100%", role: "group",
    "aria-label": "Bar chart of Google Scholar citations for the twenty most-cited publications" });
  svg.style.fontFamily = "inherit";
  svg.style.minWidth = "680px";
  svg.style.display = "block";

  // legend row (identity channel for the four threads)
  let lx = 8;
  Object.values(THREADS).forEach(t => {
    svg.appendChild(el("rect", { x: lx, y: 6, width: 13, height: 13, rx: 3, fill: t.color }));
    const lt = el("text", { x: lx + 19, y: 17, fill: "#555", "font-size": 13.5 });
    lt.textContent = t.key;
    svg.appendChild(lt);
    lx += 19 + t.key.length * 7.1 + 26;
  });

  // gridlines at 50 / 100 / 150 (solid hairlines, recessive)
  [50, 100, 150].forEach(v => {
    const gx = xFor(v);
    svg.appendChild(el("line", { x1: gx, y1: PAD_T - 4, x2: gx, y2: H - 26, stroke: "#f0ece7", "stroke-width": 1 }));
    const gt = el("text", { x: gx, y: H - 10, "text-anchor": "middle", fill: "#999", "font-size": 12.5 });
    gt.style.fontVariantNumeric = "tabular-nums";
    gt.textContent = v;
    svg.appendChild(gt);
  });

  const tip = document.getElementById("pub-tooltip");
  const showTip = (d, target) => {
    tip.replaceChildren();
    const strong = document.createElement("strong");
    strong.textContent = `${d.n} citations`;
    const ttl = document.createElement("div");
    ttl.style.cssText = "margin-top:0.2rem;";
    ttl.textContent = d.title;
    const meta = document.createElement("div");
    meta.style.cssText = "color:#d9d4cf; margin-top:0.2rem;";
    meta.textContent = `${d.venue} · ${d.y} · ${THREADS[d.t].label}` + (d.note ? ` · ${d.note}` : "");
    tip.append(strong, ttl, meta);
    const r = target.getBoundingClientRect();
    tip.style.display = "block";
    const tw = tip.offsetWidth;
    let left = window.scrollX + r.left + r.width / 2 - tw / 2;
    left = Math.max(8, Math.min(left, window.scrollX + document.documentElement.clientWidth - tw - 8));
    tip.style.left = left + "px";
    tip.style.top = (window.scrollY + r.top - tip.offsetHeight - 8) + "px";
  };
  const hideTip = () => { tip.style.display = "none"; };

  CITES.forEach((d, i) => {
    const y = PAD_T + i * ROW_H;
    const by = y + (ROW_H - BAR_H) / 2;
    const bw = xFor(d.n) - x0;

    const lbl = el("text", { x: LABEL_W, y: by + BAR_H - 3, "text-anchor": "end", fill: "#3C3C3C", "font-size": 13.5 });
    lbl.textContent = d.short;
    svg.appendChild(lbl);

    // bar: square at baseline, 4px rounded data-end
    const r = Math.min(4, bw);
    const bar = el("path", { d: `M ${x0} ${by} L ${x0 + bw - r} ${by} Q ${x0 + bw} ${by} ${x0 + bw} ${by + r} L ${x0 + bw} ${by + BAR_H - r} Q ${x0 + bw} ${by + BAR_H} ${x0 + bw - r} ${by + BAR_H} L ${x0} ${by + BAR_H} Z`,
      fill: THREADS[d.t].color });
    svg.appendChild(bar);

    const val = el("text", { x: x0 + bw + 8, y: by + BAR_H - 3, fill: "#3C3C3C", "font-size": 13, "font-weight": 600 });
    val.style.fontVariantNumeric = "tabular-nums";
    val.textContent = d.n;
    svg.appendChild(val);

    // full-row hit target (>24px tall) with keyboard focus
    const hit = el("rect", { x: 0, y: y, width: W, height: ROW_H, fill: "transparent", tabindex: 0, role: "img",
      "aria-label": `${d.title}. ${d.venue}, ${d.y}. ${d.n} citations.` });
    hit.style.cursor = "pointer";
    hit.addEventListener("pointerenter", () => { bar.setAttribute("opacity", "0.75"); showTip(d, hit); });
    hit.addEventListener("pointerleave", () => { bar.removeAttribute("opacity"); hideTip(); });
    hit.addEventListener("focus", () => { bar.setAttribute("opacity", "0.75"); showTip(d, hit); });
    hit.addEventListener("blur", () => { bar.removeAttribute("opacity"); hideTip(); });
    svg.appendChild(hit);
  });

  const box = document.getElementById("citechart");
  box.appendChild(svg);
  const hint = document.getElementById("citechart-swipe-hint");
  const updateHint = () => {
    hint.style.display = box.scrollWidth > box.clientWidth ? "inline" : "none";
  };
  updateHint();
  window.addEventListener("resize", updateHint);
})();
</script>
```

## Curriculum vitae {.section-heading}

```{=html}
<div class="container" style="max-width: 820px;">
  <p style="color:#4b5563;">
    The full CV includes all peer-reviewed publications, book chapters, law
    reviews, funded grants, project reports, presentations, and courses
    taught.
  </p>

  <a class="cv-download" href="../CV/Wylie%20CV%208.2025.pdf" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
    <span class="cv-download-icon">↓</span> Download CV <span class="cv-download-ext">PDF</span>
  </a>

  <p style="margin-top:2rem; color:#6b7280; font-size:0.95rem;">
    Full publication list on
    <a href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=-EyTKLcAAAAJ&amp;hl=en" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Google Scholar</a>.
  </p>
</div>
```
